_comment:
English translation of examples/demo-deck. The zh-CN source lives inline in index.html and acts as the baseline; any key omitted here falls back to it. Values are inserted as HTML, so
// are allowed.
cover.h1:
Make a tech talk that reads
like a magazine
cover.notes: Hi everyone — today I'll show you how html-ppt works. This demo is built with it: every slide is just a few lines of HTML plus a class.
toc.c1d: Every deck is the same work over again, and 99% of it can be a template.
toc.c2d: Tokens, layouts, animations — three separate layers.
toc.notes: Why / How / Result. The three-part shape is the most reliable way to give a talk.
quote.by: — everyone who has ever fought with slide software
quote.notes: Pause here for a second. Let the line speak for itself.
stat.h3: of your slide-making time comes back to you
stat.notes: Stress the source: 10 of my own real decks, not a benchmark.
tokens.c1p: Every visual property — color, type, radius, shadow — becomes a semantic variable.
tokens.c2note: — that is the entire aurora theme.
tokens.notes: The key: base.css only ever names variables, never literal colors. Changing theme = swapping one set of variables.
chart.h2: Where deck-building time goes, before and after
chart.notes: Afterwards, writing takes the biggest share and everything else drops — which is exactly the point.
cta.lede: Copy a deck, drop in your content, press T until a theme clicks, and export PNGs when you're done.
cta.hint: ←/→ navigate · T theme · A animation · F fullscreen · O overview · S presenter
cta.notes: End on one concrete action: a single command. Don't let people leave on "I'll try it sometime".
